title: Reflection
text:
The creation of this ePortfolio has been a valuable learning experience as it made me learn to integrate technical tools, professional presentation, and self-reflection in one project. Initially, I imagined the task as primarily a webpage. Later on, I realized that it was also the matter of establishing a professional identity on the Web and demonstrating that I continued to develop in the spheres of business, analytics, and online communication.






NoteWhy this project mattered to me



This portfolio allowed me to get beyond handing in coursework to earn marks. It made me ponder on how to introduce myself to the professional world, how to structure information in an understandable way, and how to utilize technical tools in a meaningful and useful method.

title: Reflection
text:
The creation of this ePortfolio has been a valuable learning experience as it made me learn to integrate technical tools, professional presentation, and self-reflection in one project. Initially, I imagined the task as primarily a webpage. Later on, I realized that it was also the matter of establishing a professional identity on the Web and demonstrating that I continued to develop in the spheres of business, analytics, and online communication.






NoteWhy this project mattered to me



This portfolio allowed me to get beyond handing in coursework to earn marks. It made me ponder on how to introduce myself to the professional world, how to structure information in an understandable way, and how to utilize technical tools in a meaningful and useful method.

title: Reflection
text:
What I learned

1. Learning Quarto for structured web publishing
One of the most valuable parts of this assignment was learning how Quarto can be used to create a complete website from simple .qmd files. I learned how a site is built through:

page structure
YAML configuration
navigation menus
themes and styling
rendered output for deployment

My involvement with web publishing tools had been very minimal prior to this project. In doing so, I learned that Quarto can be used to create not only reports and notebooks but also to create a professional portfolio where text, code, media, and design are all housed in a single location.


2. Understanding Git and version control
Another major lesson was the importance of Git and GitHub. Instead of uploading files manually, I learned how version control supports better project management through:

tracking changes
organizing work step by step
writing meaningful commit messages
reducing the risk of losing progress

This gave me a better understanding of how professional projects are managed and how developers maintain structured workflows.


3. Improving my confidence with analytics and Python
My background has already provided me with experience with analytics tools like Excel, Tableau, Power BI, and SQL, but this portfolio helped me take that experience in a more technical direction by providing project pages with executable Python code. This site got me to reflect on the fact that analytics are not just about tools, but also about the clarity of the insights that are conveyed.


4. Combining business knowledge with digital skills
Being a student of Business Administration in International Business, I have learned the field of financial management, managerial economics, accounting, marketing and business analytics. This task allowed me to bridge that gap between the scholarly background and the real digital skills. I started to understand how business knowledge and data presentation can be collaborative.

title: Reflection
text:
Challenges I faced
Every meaningful project includes some challenges, and this one was no different.

Folder structure and file organization
At first, it was easy to underestimate how important correct file names and folder structure are. I learned that small issues such as incorrect folder names, broken paths, or missing assets can affect the whole site.


Styling and design consistency
Customizing the theme using SCSS was another challenge. It required more attention than simply selecting a template. I had to think about colors, typography, spacing, and how the site should feel professional rather than default.


Thinking beyond minimum requirements
A further challenge was moving from “just completing the task” to building something polished. The rubric made it clear that strong work should not only function, but also communicate professionalism, reflection, and purpose.






ImportantA key lesson



I learned that a professional portfolio is not just a collection of pages. It is a carefully structured presentation of identity, skills, evidence, and goals.

title: Reflection
text:
Skills I strengthened







Skill area
What I improved




Quarto
Building pages, configuring site structure, rendering content


Git and GitHub
Version control, commit workflow, deployment awareness


Web design
Layout, SCSS customization, theme integration, visual consistency


Professional writing
Clear tone, structured presentation, audience awareness


Analytical communication
Explaining projects, presenting insights, linking tools to outcomes

title: Reflection
section: How this connects to my past experience
text:
How this connects to my past experience
The skills of documentation, analysis, reporting, and professional communication have already been developed because of my academic background and the experiences of my internship. During my internships, I worked in environments where accuracy, follow-up, and organized workflows were important. This portfolio extended those habits into a digital format.
For example:

my work in financial services strengthened my understanding of accuracy and client-focused processes
my project internship developed my teamwork, reporting, and documentation skills
this ePortfolio helped me bring those experiences together into one professional platform

title: Reflection
text:
What I would improve next
This portfolio is an important step, but it is also part of a longer journey. Going forward, I would like to improve it further by:

adding more detailed analytics projects
creating richer visualizations and interactive dashboards
expanding my use of Python for business analysis
refining the design and branding even more
continuing to update the site as I gain new skills and experience


My next development goals

Build a professional online presence
Present my academic and internship experience clearly
Learn the basics of Quarto and Git-based deployment
Add more advanced Python and dashboard projects
Strengthen my personal brand through better design and storytelling

title: Reflection
text:
Final reflection
In general, this project made me realize that professional development nowadays is not only what you know, but also how you can present it. This ePortfolio has enabled me to look back at how I have advanced in business studies, analytics, digital tools, and professional communications. It also made me think hard about the way I will go in the future.
This portfolio is more than a module requirement to me now. It is also the start of a career platform that I can build on as I advance in analytics, business intelligence, and making decisions based on data.1

title: Reflection
text:
Footnotes


This reflection is based on my learning through building the portfolio, organizing content, and presenting my academic and practical development in a professional format.↩︎

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
This project presents a business intelligence style dashboard for client portfolio monitoring, lead follow-ups, and engagement tracking. It is inspired by workflow areas such as client support, portfolio monitoring, and follow-up activity, but it uses synthetic data for confidentiality and portfolio safety.






NoteData note



The client dataset in this project is synthetically generated. It does not represent real clients, real account values, or real company records. The purpose is to demonstrate analytical thinking, reporting structure, and Python-based dashboard development.1

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
This project presents a business intelligence style dashboard for client portfolio monitoring, lead follow-ups, and engagement tracking. It is inspired by workflow areas such as client support, portfolio monitoring, and follow-up activity, but it uses synthetic data for confidentiality and portfolio safety.






NoteData note



The client dataset in this project is synthetically generated. It does not represent real clients, real account values, or real company records. The purpose is to demonstrate analytical thinking, reporting structure, and Python-based dashboard development.1

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Objectives
This dashboard project aims to:

summarize key portfolio and follow-up metrics
analyze client segmentation
track lead and conversion trends over time
identify accounts that need priority follow-up
present insights in a clear and professional format

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Python setup


Code
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (10, 6)
pd.set_option("display.float_format", lambda x: f"{x:,.2f}")
np.random.seed(38)

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
section: Create a synthetic client portfolio dataset
text:
Create a synthetic client portfolio dataset


Code
n_clients = 120

client_ids = [f"C{str(i).zfill(3)}" for i in range(1, n_clients + 1)]
segments = np.random.choice(["Retail", "HNI", "Corporate"], size=n_clients, p=[0.60, 0.25, 0.15])
risk_profile = np.random.choice(["Low", "Moderate", "High"], size=n_clients, p=[0.35, 0.45, 0.20])
region = np.random.choice(["South", "West", "North", "East"], size=n_clients, p=[0.35, 0.25, 0.20, 0.20])
product_type = np.random.choice(
    ["Equity", "Mutual Fund", "Fixed Income", "Hybrid Portfolio"],
    size=n_clients,
    p=[0.30, 0.30, 0.20, 0.20]
)

aum = []
for segment in segments:
    if segment == "Retail":
        aum.append(np.random.randint(80_000, 500_000))
    elif segment == "HNI":
        aum.append(np.random.randint(600_000, 2_500_000))
    else:
        aum.append(np.random.randint(2_000_000, 8_000_000))

monthly_revenue = [round(value * np.random.uniform(0.004, 0.012), 2) for value in aum]
satisfaction = np.round(np.random.uniform(3.2, 4.9, n_clients), 2)

today = pd.Timestamp("2025-03-31")
days_since_contact = np.random.randint(3, 95, n_clients)
last_contact_date = today - pd.to_timedelta(days_since_contact, unit="D")
next_review_date = last_contact_date + pd.to_timedelta(np.random.randint(20, 75, n_clients), unit="D")

client_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Client ID": client_ids,
    "Segment": segments,
    "Risk Profile": risk_profile,
    "Region": region,
    "Product Type": product_type,
    "AUM": aum,
    "Estimated Monthly Revenue": monthly_revenue,
    "Satisfaction Score": satisfaction,
    "Days Since Contact": days_since_contact,
    "Last Contact Date": last_contact_date,
    "Next Review Date": next_review_date
})

client_df["Follow-Up Status"] = np.where(
    client_df["Days Since Contact"] > 60, "Overdue",
    np.where(client_df["Days Since Contact"] > 30, "Due Soon", "On Track")
)

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Dashboard KPIs


Code
total_clients = len(client_df)
total_aum = client_df["AUM"].sum()
avg_satisfaction = client_df["Satisfaction Score"].mean()
overdue_rate = (client_df["Follow-Up Status"].eq("Overdue").mean() * 100)

kpi_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "KPI": [
        "Total Clients",
        "Total AUM",
        "Average Satisfaction Score",
        "Overdue Follow-Up Rate (%)"
    ],
    "Value": [
        total_clients,
        round(total_aum, 2),
        round(avg_satisfaction, 2),
        round(overdue_rate, 2)
    ]
})

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Priority review list


Code
client_df["Priority Score"] = (
    client_df["Days Since Contact"] * 0.5
    + (5 - client_df["Satisfaction Score"]) * 20
    + (client_df["AUM"] / client_df["AUM"].max()) * 25
)

priority_clients = client_df.sort_values("Priority Score", ascending=False)[[
    "Client ID",
    "Segment",
    "Region",
    "AUM",
    "Satisfaction Score",
    "Days Since Contact",
    "Follow-Up Status",
    "Priority Score"
]].head(10)

priority_clients["Priority Score"] = priority_clients["Priority Score"].round(2)
priority_clients

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Visual 1: client count by segment


Code
segment_counts = client_df["Segment"].value_counts().sort_index()
ax = segment_counts.plot(kind="bar")
ax.set_title("Client Count by Segment")
ax.set_xlabel("Segment")
ax.set_ylabel("Number of Clients")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 1: Distribution of clients across portfolio segments.

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Visual 2: total AUM by segment


Code
aum_by_segment = client_df.groupby("Segment")["AUM"].sum().sort_values()
ax = aum_by_segment.plot(kind="barh")
ax.set_title("Total AUM by Segment")
ax.set_xlabel("AUM")
ax.set_ylabel("Segment")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 2: Total assets under management by client segment.

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard

Code

ax = pipeline_plot.plot(marker="o")

Figure 4: Distribution of follow-up health across the client base.

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Interpretation of findings






ImportantKey insights




Retail clients form the largest share of the client base, but HNI and Corporate accounts contribute strongly to total AUM.
A dashboard like this helps prioritize outreach by combining relationship freshness, client value, and satisfaction.
The monthly pipeline trend suggests that follow-up activity and conversions improve when lead generation stays consistent.
Overdue review cases can be isolated quickly through a priority score, improving relationship management and service planning.

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Business value of the dashboard
This project shows how a simple analytics dashboard can support:

relationship management
review scheduling
follow-up planning
performance reporting
decision support for portfolio and sales activity

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Skills demonstrated

Python with pandas and numpy
synthetic business dataset creation
KPI reporting
dashboard thinking
prioritization logic
data visualization with matplotlib
professional communication of insights

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard
text:
Conclusion
This project reflects how data can be organized into a practical decision-support tool. Even with synthetic data, the dashboard demonstrates how analytics can improve visibility into portfolio activity, follow-up quality, and client engagement outcomes.

title: Client Portfolio and Sales Insights Dashboard

Footnotes


Synthetic data is used to protect confidentiality while still demonstrating the reporting and dashboard workflow clearly.↩︎

KYC documentation and account verification

responding to client queries related to trading and investment products

This experience improved my understanding of financial services, compliance processes, and customer support in a professional environment.

supporting the rollout of Project Helios

improving coordination within a cross-functional team

participating in problem-solving and documentation

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
This project demonstrates how Python can be used to support financial statement analysis through a structured comparison of selected 2020 and 2021 values. It reflects the financial analysis theme in my academic project work and presents the approach in a portfolio-friendly format.






NoteData note



The dataset used in this page is an illustrative academic dataset created for portfolio demonstration. It is designed to show the analytical method clearly through executable Python code, comparative analysis, common-size analysis, and ratio calculations.1

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
This project demonstrates how Python can be used to support financial statement analysis through a structured comparison of selected 2020 and 2021 values. It reflects the financial analysis theme in my academic project work and presents the approach in a portfolio-friendly format.






NoteData note



The dataset used in this page is an illustrative academic dataset created for portfolio demonstration. It is designed to show the analytical method clearly through executable Python code, comparative analysis, common-size analysis, and ratio calculations.1

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Objectives
The main goals of this project are to:

compare selected financial statement items across two years
calculate absolute and percentage changes
prepare a common-size style interpretation
calculate key financial ratios
communicate findings using tables and charts

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Analytical approach
This project uses the following ideas:
\[
\text{Percentage Change} = \frac{\text{New Value} - \text{Old Value}}{\text{Old Value}} \times 100
\]
\[
\text{Common-Size Ratio} = \frac{\text{Statement Item}}{\text{Base Amount}} \times 100
\]
For the income statement, the base amount is Revenue.
For the balance sheet, the base amount is Total Assets.

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Python setup


Code
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (10, 6)
pd.set_option("display.float_format", lambda x: f"{x:,.2f}")

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Create the financial dataset


Code
income_statement = pd.DataFrame({
    "Item": ["Revenue", "Operating Profit", "Profit Before Tax", "Net Profit"],
    "2020": [52300, 17800, 15200, 11750],
    "2021": [56900, 19250, 16540, 12820]
})

balance_sheet = pd.DataFrame({
    "Item": [
        "Current Assets",
        "Non-Current Assets",
        "Current Liabilities",
        "Non-Current Liabilities",
        "Equity"
    ],
    "2020": [29500, 39200, 14600, 9900, 44200],
    "2021": [31850, 40950, 15400, 10300, 47100]
})

Income statement comparison


Code
income_comparative = income_statement.copy()
income_comparative["Absolute Change"] = income_comparative["2021"] - income_comparative["2020"]
income_comparative["% Change"] = (
    income_comparative["Absolute Change"] / income_comparative["2020"] * 100
).round(2)

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Common-size income statement


Code
income_common_size = income_statement.copy()
income_common_size["2020 Common Size %"] = (
    income_common_size["2020"] / income_common_size.loc[income_common_size["Item"] == "Revenue", "2020"].iloc[0] * 100
).round(2)
income_common_size["2021 Common Size %"] = (
    income_common_size["2021"] / income_common_size.loc[income_common_size["Item"] == "Revenue", "2021"].iloc[0] * 100
).round(2)

income_common_size[["Item", "2020 Common Size %", "2021 Common Size %"]]

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Common-size balance sheet


Code
total_assets_2020 = balance_sheet.loc[balance_sheet["Item"].isin(["Current Assets", "Non-Current Assets"]), "2020"].sum()
total_assets_2021 = balance_sheet.loc[balance_sheet["Item"].isin(["Current Assets", "Non-Current Assets"]), "2021"].sum()

balance_common_size = balance_sheet.copy()
balance_common_size["2020 Common Size %"] = (balance_common_size["2020"] / total_assets_2020 * 100).round(2)
balance_common_size["2021 Common Size %"] = (balance_common_size["2021"] / total_assets_2021 * 100).round(2)

balance_common_size[["Item", "2020 Common Size %", "2021 Common Size %"]]

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Key financial ratios


Code
ratios = pd.DataFrame({
    "Ratio": [
        "Current Ratio",
        "Debt to Equity",
        "Net Profit Margin",
        "Return on Assets"
    ],
    "2020": [
        29500 / 14600,
        (14600 + 9900) / 44200,
        11750 / 52300,
        11750 / total_assets_2020
    ],
    "2021": [
        31850 / 15400,
        (15400 + 10300) / 47100,
        12820 / 56900,
        12820 / total_assets_2021
    ]
})

ratios["2020"] = ratios["2020"].round(3)
ratios["2021"] = ratios["2021"].round(3)
ratios

section: Visual comparison of key income statement items
text:
Visual comparison of key income statement items


Code
income_plot = income_statement.set_index("Item")
ax = income_plot.plot(kind="bar")
ax.set_title("Income Statement Comparison")
ax.set_xlabel("Financial Item")
ax.set_ylabel("Value")
plt.xticks(rotation=20, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 1: Comparison of selected income statement items for 2020 and 2021.

ratio_plot = ratios.set_index("Ratio")

Figure 3: Year-on-year comparison of selected financial ratios.

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Interpretation of findings






ImportantKey insights




Revenue, operating profit, profit before tax, and net profit all increased from 2020 to 2021.
The company’s profitability margins remained relatively stable, with a slight improvement in net profit.
The current ratio improved slightly, suggesting a modest strengthening in short-term liquidity.
Debt-to-equity stayed controlled, indicating a balanced financing structure in this simplified model.
The balance sheet structure remained stable, with equity continuing to represent a significant share of financing.

title: Analyzing Financial Performance: A Comprehensive Study of ITC
text:
Conclusion
This project shows how Python can support financial statement interpretation in a clear and reproducible way. By combining comparative statements, common-size analysis, ratio analysis, and charts, the project demonstrates a practical approach to understanding financial performance trends.
